item.title: DOJ says it scrubbed news releases about Jan. 6 criminal cases from its website
item.description: The Justice Department acknowledges it has removed from its website news releases about criminal cases related to the Jan. 6, 2021 riot, calling the information "partisan propaganda."

item.title: Iran says obstacles remain, but acknowledges deal taking shape with U.S.
item.description: As the Trump administration signals hope for a possible deal, Iran says obstacles linger, and "frequent changes" in U.S. positions aren't helping.

item.title: Experts from Thai rescue join efforts to save 7 trapped in Laos cave
item.description: Seven Laotian villagers entered a cave five days ago searching for gold and were trapped inside after heavy rain triggered flash flooding.

item.title: Ebola death toll climbs in Congo as new infections confirmed in Uganda
item.description: The WHO chief says there have been 220 suspected deaths in the ongoing Ebola outbreak as Ugandan health authorities report two new Ebola infections.

item.title: Minibus crashes into elephant, killing 3 people in Uganda national park
item.description: Three people died and four others were injured after their vehicle collided with an elephant in Murchison Falls National Park, police said on Monday.

item.title: Suspects wanted by FBI for robbing athletes' homes arrested in Chile
item.description: The thieves stole jewelry, high-end watches and souvenirs such as sports jerseys, among other belongings, police said.

item.title: Vietnam vets say opposing Trump's arch is about being "loyal to the country"
item.description: "I think it's just disrespectful to those that I served with who didn't come back," a veteran suing to stop construction of the arch told CBS News.

item.title: U.S., Iran have agreed to broad principles of deal, Trump official says
item.description: There is broad commitment on the principles of a deal, a senior Trump administration official said, and the administration feels positive about where things stand.

item.title: Husband convicted of hiring hitman to kill NYC art dealer in Brazil
item.description: Daniel Sikkema faces a mandatory sentence of life in prison. Brent Sikkema was stabbed to death in his Rio de Janeiro townhouse in 2024.

item.title: Spraying incident in luxury mall in Japan injures about 20
item.description: Two people were seen being taken on stretchers into ambulances after, authorities said, a man sprayed a substance inside an upscale mall in Tokyo.

item.title: American Music Awards air tonight with star-studded performances
item.description: The American Music Awards celebrate fan favorites in the music world and feature performances from multiple artists.

item.title: What's open and closed for Memorial Day 2026?
item.description: Most retail stores will be open for business on Memorial Day, while post offices will be closed. Here's what to know.

item.title: What to know about a leaking California chemical tank that may explode
item.description: The tank at GKN Aerospace is estimated to contain 7,000 gallons of methyl methacrylate, a volatile chemical used to produce plastics.

item.title: Crews at Orange County chemical leak in California on "all-night mission" to test tank
item.description: More than 50,000 people in California remain evacuated due to an Orange County chemical leak that prompted Gov. Newsom to request that President Trump issue a Federal Emergency Declaration.

item.title: N.J. governor requests access to Delaney Hall ICE facility amid protests
item.description: New Jersey Gov. Mikie Sherrill is asking ICE to let her inside the Delaney Hall detention center in Newark.

item.title: 5/24/2026: Booms, Busts and Bubbles; Sculpting Evolution; The Payam Method
item.description: First, examining lessons from the Wall Street crash of 1929. Then, a report on mouse DNA and the fight against Lyme disease. And, inside the "Payam Method" for learning piano.

item.title: 5/24: CBS Weekend News
item.description: Crack in California chemical tank a positive sign, but explosion fears remain; details emerging on potential U.S.-Iran peace deal.

item.title: Living with coyotes
item.description: Once found only in parts of the West and Southwest, coyotes have dramatically expanded their range, and are now found in every state except Hawaii. Conor Knighton looks at how these animals have become part of the urban landscape in places like Chicago, and what roles they play — in history, in Native American stories, in art and in today's urban and suburban ecosystems.

item.title: Second Ebola treatment center set on fire in epicenter of disease's outbreak
item.description: Angry residents of a town at the epicenter of the Ebola outbreak in eastern Congo​ attacked and burned a tent that was part of a health center where people are being treated for the virus.

item.title: The Trail of the Fallen in New York's Hudson Valley
item.description: Charlie D'Agata reports on the trail just south of West Point Military Academy that honors America's fallen patriots.

item.title: Alleged gunman outside White House had run-ins with Secret Service, sources say
item.description: Sources identified the 21-year-old suspect as Nasire Best of Dundalk, Maryland, and documents obtained by CBS News show Best previously blocked a White House entry lane in June 2025.

item.title: Sally Field says she believes "in the resilience of our Constitution" | 60 Minutes
item.description: Sally Field memorized the First Amendment as a child. The Oscar winner says she now understands "it like never before," stressing that "this fragile thing called democracy needs to be protected."

item.title: Inside the fun, accessible method helping piano students win competitions | 60 Minutes
item.description: Payam Khastkhodaei, the son of Iranian immigrants, says he's developed a new method of teaching that has students loving their piano lessons. Now his students are sweeping national competitions.

item.title: Mister Rogers Forever Stamp wins USPS's Stamp Encore Contest
item.description: It will once again be a beautiful day in the neighborhood thanks to the votes from Pittsburghers to bring back the iconic stamp.

item.title: California Gov. Newsom declares state of emergency for Orange County chemical leak as DA launches probe into its cause
item.description: Southern California officials are desperately searching for a safe resolution to a leaking toxic chemical tank at an aerospace facility.

item.title: Iran moving World Cup training base from U.S. to Mexico
item.description: Iran had been scheduled to train in Tucson, Arizona, but it will now move its training base to Tijuana, Mexico, just south of San Diego.

item.title: Kyle Busch's cause of death revealed by family
item.description: Kyle Busch, who won more races in NASCAR's top three series than anyone in history, died suddenly on Thursday.

item.title: CBS News Radio signs off after nearly 100 years: "An American institution"
item.description: After nearly a century on the air, CBS News Radio signed off, with the final reports airing on Friday, May 22.

item.title: Ebola "spreading rapidly" in Congo with national risk "very high"
item.description: The head of the World Health Organization says Ebola has killed at least 7 people in Congo, but the U.N. agency says it knows the epidemic "is much larger."

item.title: From the archives: Mount St. Helens erupts in 1980
item.description: Mount St. Helens in Washington erupted 46 years ago, on May 18, 1980. Here's how the "CBS Evening News" covered the event.
